scenario: User asks 'create an agent that audits code quality and reports issues without modifying files.' The agent must determine mode, permissions, and required frontmatter fields.
expected_behavior: Skill loads delegation-protocol.md and worktree-control.md. Agent definition includes: mode=all, tools=Read/Glob/Grep/Bash (no Write/Edit), role description, color, temperature. Validation gate checklist is completed. Read-only enforcement pattern is included.
pass_condition: Agent definition has all required frontmatter fields, correct permissions (read-only), and includes Mandatory Initial Read enforcement text.

scenario: Controller dispatches a subagent for a research task. Subagent returns DONE_WITH_CONCERNS: 'Found 3 auth libraries but Auth.js v5 breaking changes not covered.' Controller must handle the status correctly.
expected_behavior: Controller reads the concern, determines it's an observation (not correctness issue), notes the concern, and proceeds to two-stage review. Stage 1 (spec compliance) checks if all requested libraries were compared. Stage 2 (quality) checks table readability and citation presence.
pass_condition: Controller correctly classifies DONE_WITH_CONCERNS as observation (not correctness), completes two-stage review, and produces a final verdict with the concern noted.

scenario: User asks to run two subagents in parallel: one to refactor auth.ts, another to refactor auth.test.ts. The subagents conflict because auth.test.ts imports from auth.ts. Controller must detect and prevent the conflict.
expected_behavior: Skill identifies that the two files share a dependency (auth.test.ts imports from auth.ts). Worktree control rules are applied: since files are not independent, subagents must run sequentially (not parallel). Controller sequences the tasks: refactor auth.ts first, then auth.test.ts.
pass_condition: Controller detects shared file dependency, rejects parallel dispatch, and sequences tasks correctly. No merge conflicts result.
